title: Aurora Cannabis (ACB) Q3 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Aurora reported record results across multiple metrics for Q3 ending December 31, 2024: Net revenue $88.2 million (37% growth), Medical cannabis revenue $68.1 million (51% YoY growth), Net income $31.2 million (vs $18.1 million loss prior year), Adjusted EBITDA $23.1 million (316% growth), Free cash flow $27.4 million positive, Cash position $180.2 million with no cannabis business debt. Medical Cannabis represents 77% of total revenue. International revenue surged 112%, now representing 60% of global medical cannabis revenue. Canadian medical grew 6% with continued market leadership. Gross margins expanded to 74%, up from 63% YoY. Germany benefiting from descheduling with double digit patient growth. Australia achieved No. 2 market share expanding distribution. Poland and UK both achieved record quarterly revenues. Consumer Cannabis revenue $9.9 million with intentional de-emphasis due to lower margins. Bevo Plant Propagation revenue increased 22% to $8.9 million with gross margins improving to 40% from 28%. Management outlined three key strategic pillars: leveraging EU GMP and TGA manufacturing certifications globally, sustaining medical margins through operational excellence, and achieving consistent positive free cash flow while maintaining balance sheet strength. CEO Miguel Martin emphasized the company made an intentional pivot toward medical cannabis focusing on regulated markets.
